sourceLabel: City of Lethbridge 2023-2026 Four-Year Operating Budget (adopted Nov 29, 2022)
dataStatusNote: Verified from the 2023-2026 Operating Budget: 2022 General Fund baseline $254M, with annual tax rate increases of ~5.1% — 2025 General Fund estimated at ~$295M. Lethbridge Police Service incremental funding through the four-year cycle: +$2.24M (2023), +$4.08M (2024), +$4.95M (2025), +$4.96M (2026) on top of baseline. Lethbridge runs its own municipal police service (unlike RCMP-contracted Moncton/Nanaimo) and also operates its own electric, water, and wastewater utilities — those are in the separate Utility Fund and excluded here. Per-bucket amounts in the General Fund are proportional estimates pending line-item transcription from the official 2023-2026 Operating Budget PDF.

nativeDepartments.name: Lethbridge Police Service (own municipal force — estimated ~$42M)
Lethbridge Fire and Emergency Services (integrated Fire + EMS — estimated ~$28M)
Funded by the City's separate Utility Fund (water, wastewater, and electric run as municipal utilities — paid on utility bills, not property tax)
Corporate Administration — Finance, HR, IT, Legal, Communications, Council (estimated)
Fiscal Services — debt servicing, capital from operating, reserves (estimated)
Lethbridge Public Library Board operating grant + other community grants and contingency (estimated)
